info.title: VaultSage - Public API
info.description:
## Authentication (API keys)

Most endpoints that require a logged-in user accept **one** of:

- **OAuth2** bearer access token (`Authorization: Bearer ...`), or
- **Personal API key** header: `X-Api-Key`
- **Team API key** header: `X-Team-Api-Key`

Do **not** send `X-Api-Key` and `X-Team-Api-Key` in the same request.

Some paths are blocked for API keys (allowlist). Runtime errors may include
`API_KEY_ENDPOINT_FORBIDDEN` (403) or `API_KEY_HEADER_CONFLICT` (422).

post.description: Login endpoint.
Password Recovery

get.description: Get current user.

Return user's UUID v4 as iOS app_account_token.
API casing policy: snake_case for all JSON fields across the API.

post.description: Create new user.

get.description: Verify user email address via token from verification email.

post.description: Resend email verification email by email address (unauthenticated).

get.description: Get user's current tier, features, and subscription information.
Get user's direct subscriptions.

Get activity logs related to the current user.

This includes activities where the user was either the actor or the target,
such as team invitations received, role changes, file operations, etc.

**Pagination:**
- `page`: Page number (1-indexed, default: 1)
- `page_size`: Items per page (1-100, default: 20)

**Filtering:**
- `activity_type`: Filter by activity type category (optional)
  - `invite` - All invitation-related activities
  - `member` - Member management (enable/disable/remove/leave)
  - `role` - Role changes and ownership transfer
  - `file` - File operations (upload/delete/move/rename/copy)
  - `folder` - Folder operations (create/delete/rename/move)
  - `auth` - Authentication events (signup/login)
- `date_from`: Filter activities from this date (ISO format, optional)
- `date_to`: Filter activities until this date (ISO format, optional)

**Activity Types:**

*Team Management:*
- `team.invite_accept` - You accepted a team invitation
- `team.invite_decline` - You declined a team invitation
- `team.member_leave` - You left a team
- `team.role_change` - Your role was changed
- Other team events where you were involved

*File Operations:*
- `file.upload` - You uploaded a file
- `file.delete` - You deleted a file
- `file.move` - You moved a file
- `file.rename` - You renamed a file
- `file.copy` - You copied a file to Personal Space

*Folder Operations:*
- `folder.create` - You created a folder
- `folder.delete` - You deleted a folder
- `folder.rename` - You renamed a folder
- `folder.move` - You moved a folder

*Authentication:*
- `user.signup` - You created your account
- `user.login` - You signed in

Note: This returns activities where you are the target (e.g., invitation received)
or the actor (e.g., uploading files). For full team activity logs, use
`GET /team/activity-log` (requires admin role).

Stream file content for preview.
Supports both personal files and team files.

**Improved Features:**
- **Streaming**: Downloads file in 1MB chunks to reduce server memory usage.
- **Standard Headers**: Includes Content-Length, ETag, and Last-Modified for better client-side file handling.
- **Authentication**: Requires a valid user token.
- **Lazy Backfill**: Automatically populates missing `checksum` and `file_size` in the background.

Check PNG ZIP availability without downloading.
Supports both personal files and team files.

This endpoint checks if a PNG ZIP file exists for the given file ID.
Use /png-preview-download/{file_id} to actually download the ZIP.

Download PNG ZIP file for preview.
Supports both personal files and team files.

This endpoint streams the PNG ZIP file directly from GCS private bucket.
Frontend will receive the ZIP file and extract PNGs client-side.

post.description: Trigger PNG preview reprocessing for a file.

get.description:
Check PNG ZIP availability for anonymous users via share link.

This endpoint mirrors /png-preview-check/{file_id}, but authorizes access using
share_code (+ optional password/email) instead of a logged-in user session.

Download PNG ZIP for anonymous users via share link.

Note: This is a PREVIEW download. The allow_download flag is enforced by /api/v1/share/download.

Stream file preview for anonymous users via share link.

This endpoint allows anonymous users to preview files that have been shared with them.
If file_id is provided, it validates that the file is included in the share.
If file_id is not provided, it returns the first file in the share.

Now supports email verification for enhanced security (backward compatible).

Search files and directories with Team Folder support, filtering, and sorting.

Searches both personal files and files in Team Folders that the user has access to.

**Search Scope:**
- Personal files owned by the user
- Team files in any team the user is an enabled member of

**Search Fields:**
- File name (case-insensitive)
- File body content (full-text search)
- Directory name (case-insensitive)

**Enhanced Metadata:**
- Each file in the result includes `file_size`, `mime_type`, and `checksum` (MD5).

**Filtering:**
- **File Type**: Filter by file category (PDF, Image, Video, Doc, Sheets, Slides, Folder)
- **Time Range**: Filter by creation/update time (any_time, last_7_days, last_30_days)
- **Sharing**: Filter by sharing status (shared, not_shared)

**Sorting:**
- **Sort By**: name, size, last_modified
- **Sort Direction**: asc (ascending), desc (descending, default)

post.description:
Scan the database for active files belonging to the current user and regenerate their snapshots.

This endpoint will:
1. Find all active files (where status != "deleted") belonging to the current user
2. Create a task for each file to regenerate its snapshot

Returns information about the total number of files processed and tasks enqueued.

post.description: Deletes selected files. Updates database records with 'deleted' status and removes objects from GCS. **Team Folder Support:** For team files, owner/admin or file uploader can delete. Team must not be archived. **Permission Roles:** Owner/Admin can delete any file; File uploader can delete files they uploaded; Members cannot delete files uploaded by others.

404.description: One or more requested files not found

example.detail: One or more files not found

put.description:
Update a file name (rename).

If the new name would create a duplicate in the same directory,
returns a 500 error.

patch.description:
Move a file to a different directory with Team Folder support.

This updates the database records by changing the primary FileDirectoryMapping.
For Personal → Team moves, also updates file ownership and moves the GCS object.

**Team Folder Rules:**
- Team → Team (internal): Admin or Owner (uploaded_by) can move
- Team → Personal: FORBIDDEN (cannot move files out of Team Folder)
- Personal → Team: All enabled members can move personal files into Team Folder
- Personal → Personal: Owner can move

**Team File Permission Roles:**
- Admin: Can move ANY file within Team Folder
- Owner (uploaded_by): Can move files they uploaded
- Member: Cannot move files uploaded by others

**Access Control:**
- Moving personal files: Owner only
- Moving team files: Admin or file owner (uploaded_by)

**Error Codes:**
- 404: File or target directory not found
- 403: Insufficient permissions
- 400: Invalid move operation (same directory, Team→Personal, etc.)
- 500: Duplicate file name in target directory or GCS failure

post.description: Manually trigger reprocessing of files with intelligent retry capabilities. Supports different modes: full reprocessing, intelligent retry (skip completed stages), or specific stage retry. Useful for fixing failed processing or upgrading to newer processing versions.

example.message: Reprocessing initiated for 2 out of 3 files

results.message: File queued for intelligent retry processing

example.detail: Invalid processing mode or file IDs

patch.description:
Update chat file associations

Args:
    chat_id: Chat UUID
    request: File IDs to associate
    session: Database session
    current_user: Authenticated user
    
Returns:
    Updated chat information with file list
    
Raises:
    HTTPException: 
        - 404: Chat not found or access denied
        - 403: One or more files belong to another user (permission denied)
        - 422: One or more files don't exist, are deleted, or unavailable

post.description: Unified share file access verification with backward compatibility

get.description:
Get access logs for a specific share.

**Modification History**:
- **Tue Aug 19 21:00:17 CST 2025**: New API endpoint created
  - Implemented SharedFileAccess tracking retrieval system
  - Added pagination support for access logs
  - Integrated owner-only access control
- **Tue Jan 21 16:30:00 CST 2025**: Enhanced API documentation
  - Added guidance on obtaining share IDs via /api/v1/share/user-shares endpoint

**Access Control**:
- Only the owner of the share can view access logs
- Requires authentication

**Getting Share IDs**:
- To obtain available share IDs, use the `/api/v1/share/user-shares` endpoint
- This endpoint returns all shares created by the authenticated user
- Each share object contains the `id` field which can be used as the `share_id` parameter

**Tracked Activities**:
- `view`: When recipients verify share access permissions
- `download`: When recipients download files (with email tracking)

**Response Data**:
- Accessor email and timestamp
- IP address and user agent information
- Access type and specific file IDs accessed
- Session information and share code used

**Pagination**: Supports page-based pagination (default: 10 items per page)

**Error Handling**:
- **404 Not Found**: Share not found. Possible reasons include:
  - The user does not have access to the specified share ID
  - The JWT token is invalid or expired
  - The share has been deleted or does not exist
- **401 Unauthorized**: Authentication required - valid JWT token must be provided

**Important Notes**:
- **Pagination Handling**: Large number of records require using pagination functionality
- **Permission Check**: Only share owners can view access records
- **Time Sorting**: Records are sorted by access time in descending order (newest first)
- **Data Retention**: Record retention period should be determined according to privacy policy
- **Security Monitoring**: Use for detecting unusual IP addresses or frequent access patterns
- **Compliance Reporting**: Suitable for generating access reports for audit purposes

200.description: Successfully retrieved access logs for the share

example.detail: Share not found. Possible reasons: the user does not have access to this share ID, or the JWT token is invalid/expired.

delete.description:
Delete specific access log records.

**Modification History**:
- **Tue Aug 19 21:00:17 CST 2025**: New API endpoint created
  - Implemented bulk deletion of SharedFileAccess records
  - Added ownership validation for security
  - Integrated privacy compliance support (GDPR)

**Access Control**:
- Only the owner of the share can delete access logs
- Requires authentication
- Validates ownership before deletion

**Use Cases**:
- Data cleanup and maintenance
- Privacy compliance (GDPR, etc.)
- Remove test or accidental access records

**Request Format**:
```json
{
    "access_ids": ["uuid1", "uuid2", "uuid3"]
}
```

**Response**: Returns count of successfully deleted records

**Important Notes**:
- **Bulk Operation**: Can delete multiple access records in a single request
- **Irreversible Action**: Deleted records cannot be recovered
- **Ownership Validation**: System validates that all specified records belong to shares owned by the requesting user
- **Privacy Compliance**: Use for GDPR right to erasure or data retention policy compliance
- **Audit Trail**: Consider logging deletion actions for compliance purposes
- **Empty Request Handling**: Returns error if access_ids array is empty

post.description: Smart upload files with AI-driven folder organization.
Check and suggest optimal folder placement for a list of files.

get.description:
Get comprehensive dashboard metrics for the current user.

Includes storage usage, file counts by type, shared link counts, 
and recent activity.
Get all available subscription plans with platform-agnostic information.
Get team information including seat usage and subscription status.
Get all team members with their status and details.

post.description: Invite one or more members to the team. Admin only. Requires active subscription.

patch.description: Enable or disable a team member. Admin only. Requires active subscription.

delete.description:
Permanently remove a member from the team. Admin only. Requires active subscription.

**Note**: Frontend UI visibility controlled by `SHOW_TEAM_LEAVE_REMOVE_UI` setting.
API always works regardless of UI setting.

patch.description:
Change a team member's role between 'admin' and 'member'.

**Owner only.** Only the team owner can change member roles. Requires active subscription.

**Restrictions:**
- Cannot change owner's role (must use transfer ownership)
- Can only change role for ENABLED members (not PENDING or DISABLED)
- Valid roles: 'admin', 'member'

post.description:
Leave the team voluntarily. Member only (Admin cannot leave).

**Note**: Frontend UI visibility controlled by `SHOW_TEAM_LEAVE_REMOVE_UI` setting.
API always works regardless of UI setting.

get.description:
Get all pending team invitations for the current authenticated user.

This endpoint should be called after login to check if user has any
pending invitations that require confirmation.

post.description:
Accept a pending team invitation and become an active member.

After accepting:
- TeamMember status changes from 'pending' to 'enabled'
- User's team_id and team_role are set
- User gains access to team resources

Decline a pending team invitation.

After declining:
- TeamMember record is deleted
- Seat is released back to the team
- User remains without team membership

Cancel a pending invitation and release the seat.

**Admin only.** Can only cancel PENDING invitations. Requires active subscription.

Resend invitation email and reset the expiry timer.

**Admin only.** Can only resend PENDING invitations. Requires active subscription.
Rate limited: 24 hours between resends.

get.description:
Get activity logs for the team. **Requires team Admin or Owner role**.

Activity logs track team-related events like member invitations, role changes,
and member departures. Logs are sorted by creation time (newest first).

**Pagination:**
- `page`: Page number (1-indexed, default: 1)
- `page_size`: Items per page (1-100, default: 20)

**Filtering:**
- `activity_type`: Filter by activity type (optional)
  - `invite` - All invitation-related activities
  - `member` - Member management (enable/disable/remove/leave)
  - `role` - Role changes and ownership transfer
  - `file` - File operations (upload/delete/move/rename/copy)
  - `folder` - Folder operations (create/delete/rename/move)
  - `auth` - Authentication events (signup/login)
- `actor_email`: Filter by who performed the action OR target (optional)
- `date_from`: Filter activities from this date (ISO format, optional)
- `date_to`: Filter activities until this date (ISO format, optional)

**Activity Types:**

*Team Management:*
- `team.member_invite` - New member invited
- `team.invite_accept` - Member accepted invitation
- `team.invite_decline` - Member declined invitation
- `team.invite_expired` - Invitation expired (system event)
- `team.invite_cancelled` - Admin cancelled invitation
- `team.invite_resend` - Admin resent invitation
- `team.member_enable` - Member access enabled
- `team.member_disable` - Member access disabled
- `team.member_remove` - Member removed by admin
- `team.member_leave` - Member left voluntarily
- `team.role_change` - Member role changed
- `team.ownership_transfer` - Ownership transferred

*File Operations:*
- `file.upload` - File uploaded to team space
- `file.delete` - File deleted from team space
- `file.move` - File moved within team space
- `file.rename` - File renamed
- `file.copy` - File copied from team to personal space

*Folder Operations:*
- `folder.create` - Folder created in team space
- `folder.delete` - Folder deleted from team space
- `folder.rename` - Folder renamed
- `folder.move` - Folder moved within team space

*Authentication:*
- `user.signup` - Member created account
- `user.login` - Member signed in

**System Events:**
- For automated events (like invitation expiry), `actor_email` is "system"
Get team storage usage details.

post.description: Copy a file from Team Space to Personal Space.
Create a new smart organizer.
Async generate: enqueue background job and return job_id immediately.
Phase 2 (Apply): enqueue background job for file assignments based on the finalized tree.
Materialize: create/reuse Directory subtree and update file_directory_mapping (async).

get.description: Return whether the left tree toolbar container (Structure actions + Apply) should be shown.

post.description: Retry a failed apply job. Reuses the same job_id and only reprocesses unfinished batches.

get.description: Get apply progress from Redis.
Get materialize progress from Redis (best-effort).

V2: Lazy-load tree children for an editable SmartOrganizer.

- parent_id omitted/null means root-level nodes.

post.description: V2: Reorder siblings. `node_id` is kept for path stability; ordering is driven by payload.

get.description:
Get current user's onboarding state.

`should_show_tour` is true when:
- User has not provisioned sample files yet
- User has no existing files
- At least one category exists in the live release

This endpoint is safe to call on every Files page load; it is fast
(one index lookup + one count query).

List available sample file categories for user selection in the tour guide.

Only returns categories from the current live release.
Ordered by `display_order ASC`, then `created_at ASC`.

post.description:
Provision selected sample file categories to the current user's Files space.

Creates:
- `Home > Sample Files` root directory (if not exists)
- `Home > Sample Files > {Category Name}` subdirectory
- Copies all template files (with metadata) from System User

V1: Only the first category_code is processed. API accepts array for future multi-select.

After provisioning, the client should:
- Redirect to `/files/{provisioned_directory_id}`
- Auto-open Smart Organizer panel
- Show onboarding notification

ActivityLogResponse.title: ActivityLogResponse
ActivityLogResponse.description:
Response model for a single activity log entry.

Used in both team activity log and personal activity log endpoints.

Activity types supported:
- Team: team.role_change, team.member_invite, team.invite_accept, team.invite_decline,
        team.invite_expired, team.invite_cancelled, team.invite_resend,
        team.member_enable, team.member_disable, team.member_remove, team.member_leave,
        team.ownership_transfer
- File: file.upload, file.delete, file.move, file.rename, file.copy
- Folder: folder.create, folder.delete, folder.rename, folder.move

LinkFile.title: LinkFile

MemberStorageDetail.title: MemberStorageDetail
MemberStorageDetail.description:
Storage usage detail for a single team member.

This model provides per-member storage breakdown visible to ALL team members
(not just admin/owner) to promote transparency and self-management.

Fields:
    user_id: Member's user ID
    email: Member's email
    full_name: Member's display name
    is_admin: (Deprecated) True if role is "owner" or "admin"
    role: Member's team role: "owner", "admin", or "member"
    personal_used: Bytes used in personal space
    team_uploaded: Bytes uploaded to team folder by this member
    personal_percentage: Percentage of personal quota used (vs 500GB reference)
    is_near_limit: True if approaching personal quota warning threshold (80%)

TeamMemberResponse.title: TeamMemberResponse
TeamMemberResponse.description:
Response model for team member.

Includes additional fields for pending invitation management:
- days_until_expiry: For PENDING status, shows days until auto-expiry
- can_resend: Whether resend is allowed (24h rate limit)
- hours_until_resend: Hours remaining until resend is allowed

role.title: Role
